names.japanese_name: メタ重亜硫酸カリウム

natural_occurrence.description: A synthetic inorganic salt widely used as an antioxidant and preservative in wine, beer, and food products. Main ingredient in wine-making Campden tablets (alternative to sodium form). Part of the sulfite family (E220-E228) that release sulfur dioxide (SO₂). Known allergen causing severe reactions in sensitive individuals, particularly asthmatics.

manufacturing.description: Produced by passing sulfur dioxide (SO₂) through a solution of potassium carbonate or potassium hydroxide. The solution is concentrated and crystallized to yield potassium metabisulfite (K₂S₂O₅). Upon dissolution in water, it releases sulfur dioxide and forms potassium bisulfite. Preferred over sodium form in wine-making to avoid adding sodium.

other_uses.industrial: Used in photography, textile processing, and water treatment
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Used as an antioxidant in some pharmaceutical preparations
other_uses.household: Main ingredient in Campden tablets for home wine-making and brewing

eu.conditions: MANDATORY declaration 'contains sulfites' when total SO₂ ≥ 10 mg/kg or 10 mg/L. Temporary group ADI with safety concerns for high consumers. Maximum use levels: fresh/dried fruits/vegetables 10-2000 mg/kg.
eu.legal_reference: Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008, Annex II; EU Directive 2003/89/EC

usa.notes: MANDATORY 'contains sulfites' declaration when ≥10 ppm SO₂. Banned from fresh fruits and vegetables for raw consumption (1986).

japan.japanese_regulation_name: メタ重亜硫酸カリウム
japan.notes: Part of sulfite group; allergen labeling required

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0–0.7 mg/kg bw/day (temporary group ADI for E220-E228, expressed as SO₂)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 0.7 mg SO₂ equivalents/kg bw/day (temporary group ADI for E220-E228)

regulatory_history.event: FDA bans sulfites from fresh produce after deaths from severe allergic reactions
JECFA sets ADI of 0-0.7 mg/kg bw/day for sulfites (E220-E228)
EU Directive 2003/89/EC mandates allergen labeling for sulfites when ≥10 mg/kg or mg/L
EFSA/JECFA re-evaluate sulfites; maintain temporary ADI citing database uncertainties and concerns for high consumers

food_categories_eu.category_name: Wine, beer, cider, and fruit wine
Fresh, peeled, cut, shredded, and frozen vegetables

commonly_found_in: Wine and cider (most common - Campden tablets)
Fresh, peeled, cut and shredded vegetables

equivalents.product: Campden tablets for wine (standard tablet ~440mg metabisulfite)
servings of dried apricots (40g at 2000mg/kg SO₂)

adi_context.source_note: Based on EFSA/JECFA temporary group ADI for sulfites (E220-E228). MAJOR ALLERGEN: Affects ~1% of general population, 5-10% of asthmatics. Symptoms: difficulty breathing, wheezing, skin rashes, hives, headache, nausea, bronchoconstriction, anaphylaxis (rare). EFSA noted safety concerns for high consumers who may exceed the ADI through diet. Mandatory 'contains sulfites' labeling required. Wine bottles worldwide carry sulfite warnings. Preferred over sodium form in wine-making to avoid sodium addition.
